reviews.review_description:
this is a very fun, very funny, very silly mixed reality 3d platformer. you control your animal of choice around your room using the thumbstick, trying to reach the end of the level. if you touch the animal ("pet" it), it'll run faster for a while. it has a level editor and support for playing other peoples levels!

the level is made up of lots of random objects, like.. microwaves, washing machines, toilets, toilet paper, ironing boards.. which actually works really well in mixed reality because it makes sense for such things to be scattered around a room! 

funnily enough, earlier today i was looking at trackcraft and thought to myself "i want this, but a 3d platformer instead of toy cars". little did i know that a few hours later, this released, and by the same devs!

highly recommend. didn't think the silliness would work that well for a 3d platformer but this is still a 3d platformer at heart!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the support! And we love that first level you've shared, it's pretty challenging ;)!

reviews.review_description: Track craft was the first mixed reality vr game I played and the community levels create unlimited content - it's really exciting to see the developers making their next game and giving it away for free (at the moment) and it's clearly been built with the same frameworks they build for the racing game - except its a platformer and you can play as a chicken, a spider, or a pig! Instead of racing a car you control an animal and each animal has its own platforming mechanics - obviously its hilarious to control a virtual chicken that runs around and you pet to make faster, but the gameplay comes from the levels that you race on - except instead of driving carefully on a track you interact with objects in the levels (like an iron board that you pull the lever to make it spring up) - so instead of racing a track your racing to complete a level which requires you to control a character and solve puzzles and pet an animal to get to the finish as quick as possible. It's pretty casual to play but does require some space and for you to move around. It's still in development - and while I do prefer track craft to this title - how can I not give it 5 stars when it's free. This game is FUN and the animals are really well animated and simulated. It has a level editor which is easy to use and unlike track craft the objects aren't locked to a grid

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the support and detailed review, and we hope to see you hitting the top of the leaderboards even in Pet & Run!

reviews.review_description: I really liked this game! It’s a fun and simple and the pets are sooooo cute. The twist is that the more you pet your animal, the faster they go, which adds a cool little touch. If you like pets and racing, I’d definitely recommend giving it a try!

developer_response.body: Thank you for such a great review!

reviews.review_description: Very dadaistic run and jump game. I enjoyed the most the pig buddy, but spider was good too. But the rooster. OMG! I will never be a pilot. I liked the mechanic when you help the animal to get on the particular spots. I like the nonsensual combination of things which the track is made from. Its catchy. The music is fun, I love how its getting faster with the animal. But, guys...I want to race with a CAT! Next time :)

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! Let's see with the CAT; it's on our list, so stay tuned!

reviews.review_description: A platformer / racer / puzzle crossover. Sandbox offers unlimited fun and a source of levels!

this game looks a bit weird at first, but honestly racing those crazy animals on various types of courses is super fun

i mostly enjoy those puzzling platformer like levels and the bst thing is that you can build your own courses and let other players race them

reviews.review_title: So much better than you think
reviews.review_description:
I wasn’t too sure about playing this game but I’m so glad I did. It’s definitely not what I expected,,,,,,fun!! Lots of fun! I wanted to try it out for a few minutes but ended up playing for two hours. There are 30 pre-made obstacle courses or you can build your own. You can race for the leaderboards or go at your own pace. Some levels have an added challenge of puzzle-solving to get to the finish line. The pets are adorable. I love that there is a ghost player to chase for the leaderboard. I also enjoy getting comfy on the floor to play this game.  

Everyone should be playing this game! Check out my video on YT @misschiefvr

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the support and lovely video! Pretty good times in the leaderboards, too ;)

reviews.review_description:
This is one of those games which I hope takes off like the rooster. It deserves to as it’s just good fun to play. Thanks developers and keep being whacky!
This is a great opportunity to emulate Pink Floyd who put a pig over Battersea, power station to advertise the Animals album ……it broke free! where are you gonna put your rooster?

developer_response.body: Thank you so much! Good idea for the promotion; let's see if we can make it happen :)!

reviews.review_description:
This game might look a bit wierd at first, but the gameplay is more interesting then you might think.

There are 3 types of levels - racing ones, where it is mostly about getting the best time, platforming/collectible ones, where you need to jump from platform to platform.

But my personal favorite is the third type - puzzle levels. In these, you have to really think and basically build your way to the level finish while using whatever you find lying around, building crazy structures and then trying to run over them with that crazy rooster is just hilarious, but challenging at the same time. There's not so much of these puzzling levels in arcade, but there's already a lot of cool ones created by the community.

The only negative from my perspective is the ground play - sometimes it is just hard to get into a comfy position with how the levels are placed in front of me. But i found an easy solution for this. Just go to setting and set floor level to desired position. I really love how the game looks on the tabletop when you adjust it this way.
